pages.raw_text:
The committee of councilmen
and citizens appointed to see what
can be done in the matter of pro-
curing union passenger depot
held its first meeting at 1870 this
afternoon at the council chamber.


The meeting was convened on
the call of the committee chairman,
Councilman Peter Nelson, for the
purpose of organizing and discuss
ing the preliminary steps. Later
hearings will be held at which the
representatives of the different
railroads will be asked to be pres
ent, when the real problem sur
rounding the establishment of a
union depot will be gone into.


The special committee consists
of Councilmen Nelson of the Fifth
ward, Councilman Schiller of the
Third, Councilman Funk of the
First, Councilman Mohr of the
Fourth and Councilman Gray Of the
Second, Frank r. Post, J. j.
Browne, r. B. Patterson, l.. M. Day.
export and Fred E. Baldwin.
